titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD 4.3.Sacr.1 (previously, Misc. Ch. 715)

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=63 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-06 15:19:33.959939+00 | translation moa=64 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-06 15:19:33.94762+00

p.cl: Willelmus de uavs omnibus audientibus ⁊ uidentibus has literas salutem .

Sciatis me dedisse ⁊ concessisse ⁊ hac mea karta confirmasse deo ⁊ beato cudberto ⁊ monachis dunelmensibus ibidem seruientibus deo ad in
firmariam eorum duas bouatas terre in teritorio de goliu scilicet tres
percatas que iacent iuxta corftum sancti Iohannis uersus occidentem .
⁊ duodecim acras una percata minus que iacent inter terram Gillelmi
filii arkabaldi ⁊ le wingate ⁊ tresdecim acras ⁊ tres percatas que iacent inter
mare ⁊ illas tres acras que pertinent a la sergaunterie de goliu ab aquilone
cum pastura quantum pertinet ad duas bouatas terre in puram ⁊ perpetuam ele
mosinam liberam ⁊ quietam ab omni seculari seruicio consuetudine ⁊ exactione . Hanc
autem donacionem feci pro salute anime mee ⁊ pro animabus patris mei ⁊ matris
mee ⁊ heredum meorum ⁊ omnium parentum meorum . Quare uolo ⁊ firmiter percipio .
quatenus hec mea elemosina adeo sic libera ⁊ quieta sicud aliqua elimosina
liberius ⁊ quiecius tenetur ⁊ possidetur in episcopatu sancti andree.

Hiis testi
bus . Radulfo priore de coldingham godardo ⁊ gumero capellanis galtero de linde
sai huberto de uavs . Ricardo filio micaelis . Simone clerico . Ricardo sen auer . Ri
cardo filio Selburc . Nicolao de golin . galtero de edenham . ⁊ Multis aliis .

div.p: William de Vaux to all who hear or see these letters : greeting. Know that I have given and granted and by this my charter made firm to God and to blessed Cuthbert and to the monks of Durham serving God there, for their infirmary, two oxgangs of land in the territory of Gullane, that is, the three perches that lie next to St John's Croft to the west, and the twelve acres minus one perch that lie between the land of William son of Archibald and the Wingate, and the thirteen acres and three perches that lie between the sea and those three acres that belong to the sergeandry of Gullane on the north with the pasture, as much as pertains to the two oxgangs of land, in pure and perpetual alms, free and quit from all secular service, custom and exaction. This donation have I also made for the sake of the salvation of my own soul and for the souls of my father and mother and my heirs and all my forefathers. Wherefore I will and firmly enjoin, that this my alms be as free and quit as any alms is freely and quietly held and possessed in the bishopric of St Andrews. With these witnesses: Ralph the Prior of Coldingham, Godard and Gumer the chaplains, Walter of Lindsay, Hubert de Vaux, Richard son of Michael, Simon the clerk, Richard Sans-Avoir, Richard son of Selburg, Nicholas of Gullane, Walter of Ednam, and many others.
